title.purpose: The create-form heading; the largest type in the app (~22px). No oversized hero.

body.purpose: Prose/markdown viewport content in proportional sans, capped to a 72ch measure.

code.purpose: Monospace viewport content for code files; preserves column alignment.

label.purpose: Controls, author, timestamps, status — quiet 13px metadata.

shadows.name: ambient-card
shadows.purpose: The single elevation token. Lifts the content card (viewport, create form) one soft step off the canvas. Never on buttons, inputs, or rows.

motion.name: wash-fade
motion.purpose: Fades the add/remove anticipation washes in and out on viewport rows.

motion.name: morph
motion.purpose: Added lines grow in, removed lines collapse out, context lines FLIP to new positions.

motion.name: beat
motion.purpose: Duration of each morph phase (anticipate, apply); scales with playback speed.

motion.name: reduced
motion.purpose: prefers-reduced-motion: no beat, no auto-scroll, no animated height — the new state appears immediately.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The 'Animate' action in the create card — the one ember-filled element on screen.

components.name: Control Button
components.description: Square 36px playback control (prev / play-pause / next); border turns ember on hover.

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Labeled text input in the create card; inset canvas fill inside the white card.

components.name: Timeline Scrubber
components.description: Native range input across all commits: border-gray track, ember fill, white-ringed thumb. Keyboard-operable.
components.html: <input type="range" class="ds-range" aria-label="Timeline" min="0" max="10" value="4">

components.name: Code Viewport Row
components.description: The signature surface: wrapping rows mid-morph during the anticipate phase — context, an added line (green wash), a removed line (rose wash).
components.html:
1The quiet theater of the replay.
2Added lines glow, then settle.
Removed lines glow, then collapse.

components.name: Commit SHA Chip
components.description: Monospace short-SHA chip in the commit-info row above the viewport.

narrative.overview: The interface is a quiet theater built around one performance: a file rewriting itself, commit by commit. Everything that isn't the content is seating — present, comfortable, and deliberately unremarkable. The eye is pulled to the playback, never to the chrome. When a change is about to happen, the affected lines glow in a soft wash for a beat, then added lines grow in and removed lines collapse out while everything else glides to its new place. That anticipation-then-resolution is the delight, and it comes entirely from craft, not decoration. The system rejects the cold enterprise dashboard and the generic AI-SaaS landing template, and rejects flashy motion — no bounce, no elastic, no effect that doesn't clarify what changed.
keyCharacteristics: Content-first: chrome recedes so the morph is the only thing that performs.
One warm accent, used sparingly, on an otherwise neutral cool-white canvas.
Flat by default; depth appears only on the one surface that holds content.
Motion is semantic — it explains the diff, never just dresses it up.
Reading comfort (wrapping, measure, contrast) outranks visual flourish.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Ember is the only saturated color in the UI and appears on ≤10% of any screen. Its rarity is what makes 'this is the action' unmistakable. Never use it as a large background fill or as decoration.

rules.name: The Wash-Is-A-Verb Rule
rules.body: The green/rose washes are transient — they mark a line about to change, never a permanent state, and are always paired with motion and position so meaning never depends on color alone.

rules.name: The Adaptive-Type Rule
rules.body: Content font is chosen by file type: markdown/plaintext render in proportional sans (no line numbers); all code renders in monospace (with line numbers). Never force prose into monospace.

rules.name: The No-Hero Rule
rules.body: Nothing exceeds ~22px. This is a tool and a player, not a landing page; there is no display headline to shout with.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat and bordered at rest. The single ambient shadow exists only to separate the content card from the canvas. If you're reaching for a second shadow value, use a border instead.

dos: Do keep Ember (#c2410c) for interaction only — buttons, links, the timeline fill, focus rings — on ≤10% of any screen (The One Voice Rule).
Do choose the content font by file type: proportional sans for prose/markdown, monospace for code (The Adaptive-Type Rule).
Do convey add/remove with motion and position as well as the washes, so color is never the only signal.
Do keep body and muted text at AA: body/placeholder ≥4.5:1, large text ≥3:1. Muted gray (#6b7280) is the floor, not lighter.
Do draw structure with 1px hairline borders; reserve the single ambient shadow for the content card (The Flat-By-Default Rule).
Do give every animation a prefers-reduced-motion path — the morph degrades to an instant swap with no beat or auto-scroll.
donts: Don't make it feel corporate or sterile — no cold, over-formal enterprise-dashboard chrome. Warmth comes from Ember + reading type.
Don't use flashy or gimmicky motion (bounce, elastic, decorative effects). Motion must explain the diff.
Don't ship the generic AI-SaaS template: no cream/sand body background, no tiny tracked-uppercase eyebrows above sections, no hero-metric block, no endless identical card grids.
Don't use loud color, neon, or gradients; the change washes stay soft and the accent stays singular.
Don't use a border-left/border-right colored stripe as an accent; use full hairline borders or a leading chip/number.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text) or decorative glassmorphism.
Don't introduce a second saturated color or a display headline larger than ~22px (The No-Hero Rule).
